name: HNMS O 9 (P 09)
events: The history of HrMs O 9 as compiled on this page is extracted from the patrol reports and logbooks of this submarine.
This page was last updated in July 2016.

10 May 1940 In the early morning hours, HrMs O 9 (Lt. J. Metz, RNN), departed Den Helder to patrol off the Dutch coast. At 0636 hours a radio message is received that Holland is at war with Germany. O 9 remains on patrol. (1)
11 May 1940 Late in the evening HrMs O 9 (Lt. J. Metz, RNN) enters Den Helder harbour. (1)
12 May 1940 In the evening, HrMs O 9 (Lt. J. Metz, RNN), departed Den Helder together with HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) to make for England. They were escorted by the tug Witte Zee. (1)
14 May 1940 HrMs O 9 (Lt. J. Metz, RNN) arrived at the Downs. (1)

15 May 1940 HrMs Willem van der Zaan (Lt. Cdr. H.D. Lindner RNN), HrMs O 9 (Lt. J. Metz, RNN), HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)), HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. G. Koudijs, RNN) and HrMs O 24 (Lt.Cdr. G.B.M. van Erkel, RNN) all departed the Downs for Porstmouth where they arrived the next day. (1)
29 May 1940 HrMs O 9 (Lt. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) departed Portsmouth for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the English Channel to the south of the Isle of Wight.
For the daily positions of HrMs O 9 during this patrol see the map below.
1 Jun 1940 HrMs O 9 (Lt. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) ended her 1st war patrol at Portland.
At Portland she is assigned to A/S training duties. (2)

9 Jul 1940 HrMs O 9 (Lt. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) departed Portland for Rothesay. She made the passage together with HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)) and HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. G.B.M. van Erkel, RNN). This later submarine was coming from Portsmouth.
The submarines were escorted by the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 6 (Lt.Cdr. A.C. Lunbeck, RNN) during this passage. (1)

11 Jul 1940 The Dutch submarines HrMs O 9 (Lt. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN), HrMs O 10 (Lt. G. Quint, RNN(R)), HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. G.B.M. van Erkel, RNN) and their escort the Dutch torpedo boat HrMs Z 6 (Lt.Cdr. A.C. Lunbeck, RNN) arrive at Milford Haven. (1)

20 Dec 1941 HrMs O 9 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) departed Rothesay for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Bay of Biscay. During the passage south through the Irish Sea she was escorted by HMS Angle (T/Lt. E. Playne, RNVR).
Late in the evening O 9 was recalled and ordered to return to Rothesay. (8)
21 Dec 1941 HrMs O 9 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) ended her 3rd war patrol at Rothesay. (8)

16 Nov 1944 HrMs O 9 (Lt. S.H. de Boer, RNN) conducted exercises off Dundee.
Due to the many defects and the age of the boat it was decided to decommission HrMs O 9. (18)
27 Jun 1945 O 9 departed Dundee for Rotterdam. (20)
30 Jun 1945 O 9 arrived at Rotterdam where she was used to provide electrical power. (20)
